summary.intent: Implement task #267: a TreeView agnostic core component with DnD reorder variant for the ars-ui Rust UI library, following the project's spec-first, TDD workflow.
summary.outcome: Full TreeView machine implemented (Key identity, expand/collapse, selection, active-descendant keyboard nav, typeahead, DnD agnostic surface, controlled-prop syncing), with 103 unit tests, 26 snapshots, spec-conformance, proptest, ~99% coverage, two Codex review rounds addressed (19 findings), and PR #695 opened.

repo: TreeCollection::nodes() returns visible-only nodes (iterates visible_indices), not all nodes — ExpandAll requires new all_keys()/all_nodes() accessors that walk the full backing store.

Key does not implement Borrow<str>, so spec's String-based node identity (BTreeSet<String>, .contains(id)) does not compile against BTreeSet<Key> — all sibling components use Key and the spec needed correcting.
selection::State::select already rejects disabled keys internally, but init must seed disabled_keys from TreeItem.disabled flags or the guard is silently bypassed.
Active-descendant focus pattern is used (root tabindex=0 + aria-activedescendant + stable node DOM ids), not roving tabindex.

Every sibling navigation component (Accordion, Tabs, NavigationMenu) implements on_props_changed to sync controlled props; omitting it means controlled updates and post-reorder re-supply are silently dropped.
EffectName has a blanket impl — no manual impl needed on the Effect enum.
Callback<T> implements Clone + Debug + PartialEq (pointer identity); checkbox's on_checked_change + Effect::CheckedChange pattern is the canonical model for callback+effect wiring.
Navigation unit tests append to a single navigation.rs file (not per-component split); spec-conformance anatomy tests go in tests/spec_conformance/navigation.rs.
spec-compile-snippets is a syn::parse_file syntax-only check per block (no type resolution) — blocks only need to parse, not type-check in isolation.

DnD primitives (DropPosition, CollectionDropTarget, CollectionDndEvent, DraggableCollection/DroppableCollection) live in ars-collections::dnd; DraggableCollection::selection(&self) requires live selection state and cannot be implemented on the bare collection — that binding belongs in the adapter layer.
Adapter tasks #366/#509 own use_drag/use_drop wiring, DOM hit-testing, hover-expand timer, and DraggableCollection trait impls for Leptos/Dioxus respectively.
.agents/ and .claude/ skill files are hardlinked (same inode) — editing either updates both. CLAUDE.md is a symlink to AGENTS.md.
The component catalog has no impl-status column (parity/priority only) — nothing to flip on component completion.
cargo-mutants buffers progress to stdout; results appear incrementally in mutants.out/ rather than at completion.
TreeItem with all Option fields can use #[derive(Default)] instead of a manual impl — clippy enforces this.
Api struct requires a manual Debug impl (not derivable due to the PhantomData/generic bounds pattern used across all components).

code.finding: visible_keys() sits in the CollectionItem-bound impl block; new all_keys()/all_nodes() pure-read accessors belong in the no-bound impl<T> block (lines 134-175) so they are always available regardless of T bounds.
selection::State::select rejects keys present in disabled_keys, but the component must seed disabled_keys at init from TreeItem.disabled flags — the guard is a no-op if the set is never populated.

Full DnD primitive surface (DropPosition, CollectionDropTarget, CollectionDndEvent, DraggableCollection/DroppableCollection traits, CollectionDndMessages) lives here; Tabs already integrates reorder and is the prior-art reference.

Canonical pattern for callback+effect wiring: store Option<Callback<T>> in Props, emit a matching Effect variant in transition, execute via PendingEffect::run(ctx, props, send) which invokes the callback. SendResult carries pending_effects and state_changed.
Api<S> requires a manual Debug impl because the generic parameter prevents auto-derive; pattern is impl<S: State> fmt::Debug for Api<S> { fn fmt(...) { write!(f, "Api") } }.

workflow: TDD is mandatory: write failing tests first, confirm red, then implement, then confirm green — this is enforced by the post-implementation audit and Codex reviews.
The mandatory pre-PR gate is cargo xci --profile fast (10 gates including clippy -D warnings, all tests, spec validate, compile-snippets, insta --unreferenced=reject, coverage check-all).
Snapshot tests require explicit acceptance after first run (INSTA_UPDATE=new or cargo insta review); a PR adding .snap files gets the snapshot-reviewed label.

The mandatory post-PR step is a Codex review loop: post @codex review, poll for 👍 or findings, address findings with TDD + no coverage regression, reply inline, resolve threads, re-trigger until approved.
The mandatory pre-PR mutation gate was removed for agnostic components — surviving mutants are now triaged by Nightly CI rather than blocking the PR.
Spec sync is a first-class deliverable: every behavioral change must be reflected in the spec markdown before commit, and compile-snippets must pass.
Proptest (400 cases by default) supplements unit tests for state machine invariants — add tree_view event arbitraries and per-state assertions.
Parallel agent spawning (Explore subagent type) is the right tool for simultaneous codebase surveys that would otherwise serialize and blow the context window.

Background bash tasks with task notifications allow long-running operations (mutation gates, Codex polling) without blocking — but stale fallback wakeups can fire after a task is cancelled, causing confusion.
Pre-commit hooks re-stage all tracked modifications, collapsing intended multi-commit splits into a single commit — if a split is desired, unstage the second batch before the first commit, not after.
AGENTS.md (not CLAUDE.md) is the authoritative workflow document; changes to process go there and into the relevant skill SKILL.md (both are hardlinked to the same inode in .agents/ and .claude/).

friction: Stale fallback wakeup timers (ScheduleWakeup) fired multiple times after the mutation run was stopped, causing repeated 'continue task #267' prompts from already-obsolete background signals.
The mutation gate policy change mid-session (mutation run stopped, then user later asked to check the results) created confusion about which run was live and whether results were still relevant.
zsh array syntax for bulk GitHub API calls (inline comments per-thread) was incompatible — had to re-run with explicit bash invocation.
Intended two-commit split (feature + policy change) collapsed into one because the pre-commit hook re-staged all tracked modifications before the second commit could run.
Two full Codex review rounds (10 + 9 findings = 19 total) added significant latency; most round-2 findings were follow-ons from round-1 fixes rather than newly surfaced issues.
API 529 Overloaded error interrupted one tool call mid-session, requiring a resume.
cargo-mutants progress is buffered — intermediate polling via file reads returned empty/stale content, making it hard to gauge progress without waiting for completion notification.

The spec's String-based node identity was pervasive (events, props, api methods, variant sections §5/§6) requiring widespread find-and-replace across a large markdown file with fragile exact-match edits.
open_items: Adapter tasks #366 (TreeView Leptos adapter) and #509 (TreeView Dioxus adapter) own the remaining DnD work: DraggableCollection/DroppableCollection trait impls for TreeCollection, DOM pointer/keyboard hit-testing, 500ms hover-expand timer, and use_drag/use_drop wiring.
§5 Lazy Loading and §6 Renamable Nodes variants are intentionally out-of-scope (rust,no_check blocks in spec) — deferred to future tasks.

The original mutation run (stopped before policy change) found 6 MISSED + 2 timeout mutants; killer tests were added for all 8, but the re-run confirming 0 missed was stopped before completion — surviving mutants will surface via Nightly CI.
83 pre-existing doc warnings (crate-wide lint noise) remain unaddressed — unrelated to tree_view but visible in cargo doc output.
The third Codex review pass (after round-2 fixes were pushed) was re-triggered but the transcript ends before its verdict — that pass may surface additional findings.
